text: Extract duplicate-detection core into a tested module
description: The duplicate-detection logic (all-pairs cosine + parent-child exclusion + union-find clustering) was copy-pasted inline across project.findDuplicates and project.graphHygiene, with ZERO test coverage despite being precision-sensitive maintenance logic. Extracted it into packages/backend/src/duplicate-detection.ts (isDirectParentChild, findDuplicatePairs, clusterDuplicates) — pure, dependency-injected (vectors + aimMap), behaviour-preserving — and added regression tests pinning the parent-child exclusion (the a7f10b45 precision fix) in both the ranked-pair report and the cluster union-find, plus sort order and transitive clustering.

reflections.context: Active phase was drained, so I worked graph-maintenance tooling (fe8b6496). Intended the a7f10b45 follow-up (exclude parent-child from duplicate reports) but found it already implemented in both procedures and already marked done — no drift.
reflections.outcome: Pivoted to the real gap: that precision logic was copy-pasted across findDuplicates + graphHygiene and had zero tests. Extracted a pure duplicate-detection module (vectors + aimMap injected) and added 5 regression tests, DRYing the duplication and pinning the parent-child exclusion + transitive clustering. Recorded as a done child of fe8b6496.
reflections.effectiveness: Good: verifying against code first (per the guide) stopped me re-implementing a done feature, and redirected the effort to durable value — tests where there were none, on logic that drives the whole graph-health story. Injecting vectors+aimMap made it unit-testable without disk/embeddings/tRPC, sidestepping the awkward vectors.json path seeding. Behaviour preserved (52 backend tests green, return shapes unchanged).
reflections.lesson: When an aim looks like 'implement X', check whether X already shipped — the higher-value adjacent work is often locking it in with tests + DRYing copy-paste, not re-doing it. Extracting inline logic into a deps-injected pure module is the cheapest path to coverage for code that otherwise needs heavy fixtures (here, on-disk vector store).
reflections.pattern: verify-then-redirect; extract-pure-helper-for-testability (same tactic as composeInstructContext and graphData).

status.comment: Done 2026-06-28 (commit pending). New packages/backend/src/duplicate-detection.ts with pure isDirectParentChild / findDuplicatePairs / clusterDuplicates; project.findDuplicates + project.graphHygiene refactored to call them (removed the two copy-pasted isDirectParentChild predicates + inline loops; behaviour identical, return shapes unchanged). 5 unit tests in duplicate-detection.test.ts (adjacency both directions; non-adjacent near-dup reported while parent-child excluded; score-desc ordering; clustering excludes parent-child but links transitively). Verified: backend tsc clean + 52 tests (was 47, +5); mcp tsc clean. Locks in the a7f10b45 parent-child-exclusion precision fix that previously had no coverage.
